Source URL: https://www.sba.gov/local-assistance

# SCORE Process Companion for SBDC Routing

## Why this file is included
The user requested files on the SCORE process as part of the resource-pack pattern. This SBDC pack does not re-scrape SCORE, but it includes SCORE routing logic so BOSS can decide when a Houston entrepreneur should use SBDC, SCORE, or both.

## SCORE process in BOSS terms
1. Identify whether the entrepreneur needs operator mentoring or an experienced sounding board.
2. Collect enough facts to avoid sending a vague request.
3. Prepare a short mentor handoff summary.
4. Refer the user to SCORE for mentoring while keeping SBDC as the local technical/planning resource.
5. For Houston users, use SCORE Houston or the national SCORE mentor system depending on the need.

## When to route to SCORE from an SBDC conversation
- User is overwhelmed and needs an ongoing mentor.
- User has multiple competing business ideas.
- User needs operator judgment in a specific industry.
- User needs accountability between SBDC advising sessions.
- User needs a second perspective before spending money.

## When to keep SBDC primary
- User needs market research, financial projections, business-plan structure, local lender readiness, government procurement readiness, export support, tech commercialization, or local resource navigation.
